theory.canonical_statement: The spectral density index is defined by $\eta(\omega)=\omega^{-\beta}e^{-\omega/\omega_0}$, where $\omega_0$ is a characteristic frequency fixed by $u_T$ and $\beta$ is a dimensionless exponent determined by the hyper-dimensional span.
theory.technical_interpretation: The spectral density index is defined by $\eta(\omega)=\omega^{-\beta}e^{-\omega/\omega_0}$, where $\omega_0$ is a characteristic frequency fixed by $u_T$ and $\beta$ is a dimensionless exponent determined by the hyper-dimensional span.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This sets the large-scale shape of the spectrum and controls how strongly lower frequencies are favored.
This inserts the characteristic-frequency suppression that keeps the spectrum from remaining scale-free at high frequency.
This closes the progression by combining scaling and cutoff into one spectral weighting law tied to the Trawin base unit and hyper-dimensional span.

external_sources.title: Spectral Density Index (TZP-Weighted)
external_sources.canonical_claim: The spectral density index is defined by $\eta(\omega)=\omega^{-\beta}e^{-\omega/\omega_0}$, where $\omega_0$ is a characteristic frequency fixed by $u_T$ and $\beta$ is a dimensionless exponent determined by the hyper-dimensional span.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Power spectral density) supports the definition (spectral density) by showing that The spectral density article defines the power spectrum as describing the distribution of power into frequency components of a signal.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: The spectral density article defines the power spectrum as describing the distribution of power into frequency components of a signal. It notes that the spectral density quantifies how energy or variance is distributed over frequency. This supports using a spectral density index that weights frequencies near the zero point.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.
